name: Dinosaurs
description: Paleontology terms about the giants that once ruled the Earth.

terms.definition: The geological era from about 252 to 66 million years ago, often called the Age of Dinosaurs.
A group of mostly carnivorous, bipedal dinosaurs that includes Tyrannosaurus and modern birds.
A group of huge, long-necked herbivorous dinosaurs such as Brachiosaurus and Diplodocus.
A dinosaur that fed primarily on plants, often equipped with grinding or cropping teeth.
The preserved remains or traces of ancient organisms, such as bones, eggs or footprints.
A scientist who studies fossils to understand the history of life on Earth.
The dying out of a species; a mass extinction ended the non-avian dinosaurs 66 million years ago.
